The natural `\bq3400-ra\b` is a TRAP: `\b` matches before a hyphen, so it ALSO swallows a longer SKU like `q3400-ra-x` — a rev John never ruled on would silently inherit this ruling. Caught by the pre-ship guard, not by reading. The shipped boundary excludes a trailing `-`, so only the exact SKU moves. This is the same discipline as .242's `\b` guard that deliberately keeps `sn5600` off the `sn5610`=2U row: seeding a family the owner did not rule on is precisely the guess .238 exists to kill.
